title: Pungent Braised Portuguese Lamb Stew
description: A sour and molten weeknight Portuguese dish featuring lamb stock and a low carb friendly braise, this stew is perfect for a cold evening.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, and dried oregano.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium high heat.
Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, then remove from the pot.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for an additional minute.
Add the lamb stock, red wine vinegar, and browned lamb shoulder to the pot.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
